week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Nine category wins, 8 HR, 38 K, and four hitters with OBP above .385 — this offense is operating on a different level and the pitching held up; still the class of the league | same

2. BaseballPro77 | An 8–2 demolition powered by Murakami's 3 HR, Trout's 2 HR and .650 OBP, and Stanton adding pop — the rotation is uneven but the offensive ceiling remains the highest in the league | up

3. General Hospital | A 4–4 tie stings but Brandon Lowe's 3 HR, Luis Robert Jr.'s .571 OBP, and Cam Schlittler's 0.19 WHIP confirm this is an elite two-way roster that ran into a buzzsaw and still nearly won | same

4. Albuquerque Bombers | A 6–4 win built on pitching — Alcantara and Rogers both threw quality starts with sub-1.00 WHIPs — and Sal Stewart's .875 OBP is the kind of absurdity that wins tight categories; four waiver adds add depth | up

5. North Correa | A clean 7–3 win with Arozarena scoring 5 runs, Bregman going deep twice, and Sale and Glasnow both delivering quality starts — this rotation is starting to look like a genuine strength | same

6. Athletic Park Dragons | A 5–4 win over Hiroshima masked by a 4.10 ERA and thin offense; Cristopher Sánchez's 0.50 WHIP and Joe Ryan's 0.56 WHIP are elite, but the lineup needs more consistent run production | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Six HR, five SB, and 43 K from the pitching staff in a 4–6 loss — the raw talent is undeniable but a 6.00 team ERA with zero quality starts is a rotation-wide emergency | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Tarik Skubal's 0.50 WHIP and 44 team strikeouts are genuine weapons, but a 4–5 loss despite a 2.35 ERA tells you the offense is not yet pulling its weight | same

9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 4–4 tie with seven HR and solid pitching but still no win — Brendan Donovan's 2 HR and .556 OBP were excellent and Chase Dollander's addition adds rotation intrigue, but the luck demons persist | same

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A brutal 1–9 loss despite Shea Langeliers' 3 HR, Royce Lewis going deep twice, and Jacob Misiorowski's 11 K — a 7.91 team ERA with zero quality starts is simply unsalvageable | down

11. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease's 12 K and 1.69 ERA were wasted in a 2–8 blowout; Logan Webb's 10.80 ERA and zero quality starts as a team indicate a rotation that needs an emergency overhaul | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Bryan Woo and Gregory Soto both impressed but Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA torpedoed the staff to a 3–7 loss; dropping McGreevy without a real replacement signals a roster still searching for answers | same

13. Benchwarmers | The 7–3 win is real but the underlying stats — no QS, 4.50 ERA, 2 HR — scream regression; Zach Neto carried this team and the rotation looks thin with Sheehan posting a 10.80 ERA | down

14. Buttville Skidmarks | DeLauter's 4 HR and Gausman's near-perfect outing deserved far better than a 3–7 loss; with a 6.02 team ERA and wildly inconsistent rotation, the talent keeps getting wasted by pitching volatility | same
week_02: 1. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9-0 shutout anchored by Ben Rice's monster week and Mason Miller's 0.30 WHIP; this team just announced it belongs at the top | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Most consistent team in the league across all four weeks, 8-1 category win this week, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both mashing with Chase Burns dealing — the floor here is elite | same
3. BaseballPro77 | Gutted out a 5-4 win on fumes offensively, but the roster depth and active management keep them near the top; the ERA concern is real and needs fixing fast | down
4. General Hospital | Six category wins with a 2.10 ERA and only 5 HR — this offense has legitimate power gaps but the pitching and on-base skills carry them; Kumar Rocker addition could be huge | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and a staff-wide 2.25 ERA powered a 6-4 win; tied the league K record and the rotation with Ashcraft, Luzardo, and Burke is genuinely deep | up
6. Riverside Roughnecks | Exploded for 38 runs and 10 HR in a 7-3 win — Otto Lopez, Xavier Edwards, Jackson Merrill, Kerry Carpenter all contributing; is this the breakout week they needed? | up
7. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Duran all scoring runs; the .267 OBP is alarming and 46 IP with 46 K is thin rotation usage — needs more from the arms | down
8. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went off for 4 HR and 10 RBI and they still only won 7-3; rotation outside Senga needs reinforcement and the Austin Wells addition at catcher is smart | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 26 K and 1.65 ERA was absolutely elite but Freddie Freeman's .226 OBP is deeply concerning; four waiver adds show urgency and energy from the manager | same

10. Hiroshima Radiation | Dropped 8-1 in a bloodbath despite Carlos Correa scoring 8 runs and Schwarber going deep 3 times; a 43 K staff week is the core problem and Colin Rea won't fix it alone | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6-4 but Altuve's .577 OBP, Walker, Henderson, and Díaz all went deep — the offense is real; Aaron Nola and Seth Lugo both getting torched is the recurring nightmare | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite a 3.19 ERA and 4 QS — the pitching did its job and still couldn't cover for the offense; Parker Messick and Gausman both brilliant and it didn't matter | same
13. Benchwarmers | Won 5-4 but this is a smoke-and-mirrors record; Corbin Carroll and Cody Bellinger are real pieces but the rotation logged only 46 IP and the power numbers are near-invisible | same

14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Got blanked 9-0 in every single category — zero wins, zero stolen bases, a .270 OBP, and Imanaga struggled again; the aggressive waiver moves including Masyn Winn and Dustin May signal panic mode | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Tied for the most category wins this week with 8, Michael Wacha was nearly unhittable (0.60 ERA, 0.73 WHIP), Max Muncy hit 3 HR, and the offense continues to lead the league in consistency across all three weeks — nobody has been better | same

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Taj Bradley (17 K), Tarik Skubal (14 K), and 6 quality starts powered an 8–2 beatdown; the rotation has arrived, Bryce Harper is posting a .640 OBP, and this team is no longer a dark horse — they are a genuine title contender | up

3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker erupted for 5 HR and 6 RBI, Logan Gilbert was sharp, and the team won 5–3 with balanced contributions across the board; three straight winning weeks confirm this roster is deep and well-managed | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 R, .655 OBP) and Willson Contreras (2 HR, 9 RBI, .607 OBP) were among the best performers in the entire league this week, and the 6–3 win shows the offense can carry games even when pitching is inconsistent | up

5. General Hospital | A 4–4 tie against a Buttville team posting a 2.25 ERA is painful, and the rotation's 5.48 ERA is alarming; 70 K and 5 NSVH keep the floor high, but Cam Schlittler and Michael Soroka need to stabilize for this team to reclaim their ceiling | down

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost a tight 5–4 battle despite Brice Turang posting a .474 OBP and the offense scoring 30 runs — they are clearly more competitive than their record suggests, and four aggressive waiver adds signal a manager who is not sitting still | same

7. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani posted 3 HR and a .467 OBP, Oneil Cruz stole 6 bases, and 11 stolen bases as a team is elite multi-category production; a 5–3 loss despite those numbers is frustrating, but the talent level here remains firmly top half | same

8. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 over the Benchwarmers with Trevor Story quietly posting 9 RBI and Jackson Merrill chipping in 3 SB, but zero quality starts is a structural problem that will catch up to them in a tougher matchup | down

9. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz posted 2 HR, 4 SB, and a .438 OBP, MacKenzie Gore's 0.60 WHIP was electric, and the team ERA of 2.25 was legitimately excellent — a 4–4 tie against General Hospital feels like a win in disguise | up

10. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Imanaga's 0.17 WHIP, 62 team K, and 7 NSVH are all real weapons, and the waiver additions of Chase Dollander and multiple bullpen arms show a manager retooling aggressively; the -0.60 luck delta cannot last forever | up

11. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8–2 to the Skeeters despite solid individual performances from Brandon Nimmo, Yordan Alvarez, and José Ramírez — 75 team strikeouts and a 3.99 ERA show real pitching quality, but the offense has yet to string together a complete week | down

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 to the Dragons but Kris Bubic posted a 0.43 WHIP and Brandon Woodruff bounced back — the pitching staff ERA of 1.65 is the best mark in any losing team this week and shows the rotation has genuine depth when healthy | same

13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 4 HR, deGrom posted a 1.64 ERA, and the team still got blown out 8–2; adding JR Ritchie while dropping Roki Sasaki is a puzzling move for a team that desperately needed rotation upside | down

14. Benchwarmers | The +0.91 luck delta screams unsustainability — 34 K, a 5.53 ERA, and no quality starts are the hallmarks of a team about to fall hard; facing General Hospital next week is the worst possible draw for a roster running on fumes and fortune | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI with a 2.33 ERA — this team just issued the loudest statement of the season and now faces Abbotsford-Colby Twins in a massive week-five test | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Still the most consistent team in the league across five weeks and a top-four category-wins finisher even in a loss; North Correa awaits in what could be the marquee matchup of week five | same
3. General Hospital | An 8-2 demolition backed by a 2.68 ERA, 6 QS, and a 0.96 WHIP confirms this rotation is elite; Kumar Rocker's addition makes an already-scary staff downright unfair | up

4. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Six quality starts, a 2.49 ERA, and Paul Skenes posting a 0.90 ERA — the pitching is legitimately elite and the offense showed enough life to earn a 6-3 win; the -0.41 luck delta remains a nagging concern | up
5. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge's 5 HR and a 3.01 ERA anchored a clean 5-2 win; this team is rounding into form and faces Riverside Roughnecks in a pivotal week-five clash | same

6. Albuquerque Bombers | A 5-4 win fueled by Buxton (3 HR), Stewart (3 HR), and Turang's .429 OBP shows a roster firing on multiple cylinders; four waiver adds this week signal an aggressive manager not satisfied with good enough | same

7. Hiroshima Radiation | Skubal and Eovaldi each posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA of 2.81 with 5 QS is elite, but a negative NSVH dragged them to a 6-4 loss — the saves situation is a genuine structural weakness heading into General Hospital | down

8. North Correa | Nico Hoerner's 11 RBI was one of the best individual performances of the week and Austin Wells replaces Realmuto at catcher in a smart positional upgrade; a date with Sugar Land looms as a true measuring-stick game | same

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez went 4 HR, 5 SB, and a .533 OBP in one of the best individual weeks of the season and the Twins still nearly let Hiroshima back in — a murderous week-five matchup against BaseballPro77 will reveal a lot | down

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A 4-4 tie with a weaker Canberra team is a missed opportunity; Misiorowski's 14 K and a loaded bullpen are real assets but the offense needs Kurtz and Story to deliver consistently heading into an Athletic Park showdown | same

11. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong provided offense but a 4.35 ERA and just 52 K in under 50 innings is below average; dropping Roki Sasaki for JR Ritchie is a significant gamble that could define their season | up

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero delivered 3 HR and Langford hit .450 OBP but a 4.87 ERA and Gore's 6.52 ERA in the rotation are crippling; the offense has moments but too many pitching liabilities to move up the board | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Held to just 33 hits and a .257 OBP against the hottest offense in the league, but Bryan Woo's 2 QS and Mason Miller's 4 NSVH are legitimately valuable pieces; dropping McGreevy without replacing him leaves the rotation dangerously thin | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 0.82 ERA was beautiful and Bellinger looked lively, but 4 HR, 45 K, and a paper-thin power profile make this team entirely luck-dependent — a matchup against Buttville offers a winnable path but the ceiling is still very low | down
week_05: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 52 runs and 15 HR in a single week is a statement; this offense is the most dangerous in the league and the Skeeters remain the most consistent team across all five weeks | up

2. General Hospital | A 5-5 tie stings when you expected a win, but Wrobleski's 0.69 ERA, Fried's 0.63 WHIP, and 5 QS confirm this rotation is elite; Kumar Rocker waiting in the wings makes this even scarier | same

3. BaseballPro77 | Lost 5-3 but Murakami (3 HR), Trout (2 HR, 2 SB), and Basallo (.611 OBP) all showed up — this offense is loaded and Strahm adds late-inning reliability to a staff that needed it | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | Led the league with 13 HR and 39 RBI and still lost 6-4 because the pitching staff posted a 6.31 ERA with zero quality starts — the talent ceiling is enormous but the rotation is a genuine crisis | down

5. Albuquerque Bombers | An 8-1 demolition fueled by Harrison, Roupp, and a fully activated lineup; four waiver adds including Josh Jung show a manager aggressively patching holes, and this week proved the roster has real teeth | up

6. North Correa | Lost a heartbreaker 5-4 despite Glasnow posting a 0.25 WHIP and Sale a 0.50 WHIP — you simply cannot pitch better than that and lose; Ohtani's 3 SB and the 10-steal week show elite multi-category upside | same

7. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean's 17 K and 12 NSVH as a team are real weapons; the 5-5 tie feels like a loss given the opportunity, but the saves-and-holds structure makes this team uniquely dangerous in a close race | up

8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | A 5-3 win with Aranda (3 HR), Chisholm, and Griffin delivering is encouraging; the -0.43 luck delta continues to haunt but this roster is too talented to stay in the middle tier long | same

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-4 over a loaded Dragons squad — Kurtz (8 R, 3 HR), Suzuki (3 HR, .524 OBP), and 5 QS show a team that is quietly building something real; Ragans and Misiorowski both looked sharp | up

10. Canberra Wombats | Cal Raleigh (4 HR, 6 R) and Christian Walker (3 HR) gave the offense a pulse, but Aaron Nola's 11.00 ERA is a disaster and a team ERA of 5.59 with just 2 QS won't sustain ties, let alone wins | same

11. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The worst luck delta in the league (-0.62) is becoming an identity crisis; Harris (9 RBI), Moniak (2 HR), and solid pitching all week and they still can't get past a tie — Dustin May adds rotation hope | down

12. Buttville Skidmarks | De La Cruz and Caminero each went 3 HR and the team still only scored 24 runs; 5 NSVH is a real asset but Bradish's 2.03 WHIP is alarming and the offense is too inconsistent to trust | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got blasted 8-1 in every pitching category and the rotation gave up a 5.81 ERA; dropping McGreevy without a meaningful replacement signals a front office that doesn't have answers yet | down

14. Benchwarmers | The +1.33 luck delta is the highest in the league and the most unsustainable number in the data — 3 HR, a 5.28 ERA, and a thin power profile mean the house of cards is one bad week from collapsing | same
